results.title: Accuracy Standards of Bus ETA System over Past Three Years and Monitoring Measures of System Contractor's Performance
results.summary: The bus Expected Time of Arrival (ETA) system is designed to predict arrival times within a three-minute range at least 95% of the time. Over the past three years, actual performance met this target, though a January 2026 failure resulted from a software defect triggered by a server issue, which has since been fixed. Since 2024, the Land Transport Authority has upgraded the system to improve predictions and provide real-time information while closely monitoring reliability. Remedial actions have been taken to prevent recurrence, with the system's performance currently stabilising.

results.title: Additional Government Support for Residents Facing Persistent Disturbances from Errant Neighbours
results.summary: The Community Disputes Resolution (Amendment) Act 2024 introduces three key measures to support residents facing persistent neighbour disturbances. First, authorised officers can issue Mediation Directions requiring non-compliant neighbours to attend mediation, with non-compliance constituting an offence; this pilot in Tampines will expand nationwide. Second, the new Community Relations Unit (CRU) gains investigatory powers to issue advisories, warnings, and abatement orders, currently piloted in Tampines Town before wider rollout. Third, unresolved cases may proceed to the Community Disputes Resolution Tribunals (CDRT), where filing fees are capped at $150 with fee waivers available for those in financial need. For severe recalcitrant cases involving repeated court convictions for disobeying orders, HDB may consider compulsory acquisition of the flat. Residents facing immediate threats or safety concerns should contact the Police.

results.title: Alternative Singpass Identity Options Apart from Facial Verification
results.summary: The Government Technology Agency and Monetary Authority of Singapore confirmed that alternative Singpass verification methods, such as one-time passwords and hardware tokens, are available for users unable to use facial recognition due to medical conditions. MAS mandates that banks onboarded to Singpass must provide at least one non-facial alternative, while government digital services are tested to ensure accessibility for all abilities. The Minister acknowledged that public communication regarding these alternatives requires improvement to prevent affected users from needing to raise issues through forum letters or parliamentary questions. The Government will continue collaborating with stakeholders to expand alternative methods and enhance public education on existing options.

results.title: Alternative Biometric Verification Options for Medically Vulnerable and Mask-dependent SingPass Users
results.summary: MAS requires banks, including digital-only institutions, to offer alternative verification methods for SingPass users unable to use facial recognition due to medical conditions like nasal ventilation masks. Affected customers can contact their bank's customer service team for assistance. The Monetary Authority of Singapore mandates multifactor authentication for online banking security but expects banks to accommodate users with medical equipment that obstructs facial recognition technology.

results.title: Annual Data on Complaints to CASE for Blind Box and "Gacha" Mechanisms from 2023 to 2026 and Outcomes against Errant Retailers
results.summary: The Consumers Association of Singapore (CASE) received zero, four, and five complaints regarding blind box mechanisms in 2023, 2024, and 2025, respectively. All reported issues concerned non-fulfilment of orders or missing/defective items. None of these cases were referred to the Competition and Consumer Commission of Singapore (CCS) for investigation, as CASE resolved them directly by providing assistance to complainants. No penalties or warnings were issued to retailers in the provided data.

results.title: Appeals on Fines against Illegal U-Turns Guided by GPS Navigation Apps for Past Five Years and Solutions Taken by Police
results.summary: The Traffic Police have not tracked the number of appeals regarding illegal U-turns caused by erroneous GPS navigation prompts. The Ministry maintains that motorists must follow physical road signs rather than GPS instructions for U-turn decisions. While acknowledging the suggestion, the Police will consider notifying navigation companies upon receiving credible reports of such errors. No formal collaboration with these companies to rectify errors has been established or confirmed.

results.title: Assessment of IPS Finding that Policy Language may Portray Citizens as Recipients of Government Generosity Rather Than Stakeholders
results.summary: The Ministry of Finance agrees with the Institute of Policy Studies' finding that policy language should better recognise citizens as stakeholders rather than mere recipients. While the Ministry will continue to highlight citizen contributions and support active participation, it maintains that all support measures are funded by tax and other revenues. Most schemes are permanent, structural, and sustained, whereas only a small proportion, approximately 5% in the FY2025 Budget, are one-off. Communications will remain clear about the nature and intent of Government support across different life stages.

results.title: Assessment of Links to or Implications for Singapore from Epstein Files Saga
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for National Security and Minister for Home Affairs stated that the Police have received no information indicating potential criminal activities in Singapore linked to the Epstein Files saga. Consequently, the Government has not identified any implications for Singapore, including regarding human trafficking or sexual offences involving minors, nor are any follow-up actions currently being considered. No further assessment or specific measures have been announced by the authorities at this time.

results.title: An Industrial Policy in Finance
results.summary: Singapore must actively formulate an industrial policy for gold to capture structural shifts in global reserves driven by custody risk and political alignment, rather than relying on market interest alone. The Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) confirms it is exploring opportunities to develop the financial centre as a gold trading hub, noting that the international market allows multiple centres like Dubai to coexist without zero-sum competition. To compete with hubs such as London and New York, MAS is considering a "Sovereign Guarantee" via a Reserves Custody Protection Act or MAS Act amendment to immunise custody from executive discretion. This approach aligns with sound policy choices, discipline, and governance that have historically driven the sector's growth. MAS works collaboratively with the industry to identify specific opportunities and determine necessary policy adjustments to ensure the framework remains fit for purpose as the global landscape evolves.

results.title: Assessment of Transboundary Haze Episodes, and Review of Bilateral and Regional Haze-mitigation Measures
results.summary: Meteorological Service Singapore assesses that hotspot activity could increase due to wind patterns and drier conditions, though current 24-hour Pollutant Standards Index (PSI) readings remain in the "good" to "moderate" range with a peak of 69, well below the threshold for a haze episode under the Transboundary Haze Pollution Act. Consequently, no investigation has been triggered, and while four companies are under ongoing investigation for recent pit fires in Johor, no prosecutions have occurred in the Act's 12-year history. The Haze Task Force, led by the National Environment Agency and comprising 28 agencies, will activate health safeguards, including advisories and N95 mask distribution, only if haze levels worsen. Regional cooperation continues through the ASEAN Agreement on Transboundary Haze Pollution, with Singapore providing forecasts and capability building to neighbours without offering financial assistance.

results.title: Breakdown of Primary 3 to 6 Students Receiving Private Tuition by Subject and Median Household Spending to Evaluate Sufficiency of School-based Learning
results.summary: The Ministry of Education does not track the proportion of Primary 3 to 6 students receiving private tuition by subject. According to the 2023 Household Expenditure Survey, resident households spent an average of $104.80 monthly on private tuition. The Ministry does not use these indicators to evaluate the sufficiency of school-based learning, noting that learning outcomes are influenced by multiple factors beyond school and that reasons for taking up tuition vary.

results.title: Breakdown of Spousal Abuse Cases in 2024 by Gender and Age of Victims and Perpetrators, Types of Abuse and Referral Sources
results.summary: The Ministry of Social and Family Development does not currently track spousal abuse data by victim or perpetrator age, gender, or specific abuse type. While the Domestic Violence Trends Report 2025 records 2,136 new spousal abuse cases in 2024, granular breakdowns for these variables are unavailable. Self-referrals remain a significant source for cases managed by Family Service Centres. The Ministry is collaborating with community agencies to collect more detailed data on spousal abuse in the future.

results.title: Breakdown of Visitors to Sisters' Island Marine Park by Public Ferry and Private Craft over Past Three Years, and Initiatives to Improve Accessibility and Increase Visitorship
results.summary: Since the enhanced Sisters' Islands Marine Park opened in October 2024, over 40,000 visitors have arrived, primarily via public ferry. To improve accessibility and connectivity, a new daily boat service from Sentosa commenced in December 2025, supplementing the existing Marina South Pier route. NParks has implemented barrier-free access routes, a floating boardwalk, and wheelchair-friendly shelters within Big Sister's Island. Additionally, the National Parks Board partners with the Friends of Marine Park to organise guided nature walks and citizen science activities aimed at reducing entry barriers and increasing visitorship.

results.title: CDC Vouchers Scheme Proximity Thresholds for Heartland Merchants Onboarding Exceptions
results.summary: The CDC Vouchers Scheme excludes merchants located in commercial malls from onboarding, focusing exclusively on HDB heartland hawkers and merchants to support daily household expenses. This policy distinction was established in 2023 to balance household relief with support for local traders, resulting in over 24,000 onboarded hawkers and heartland merchants as of the latest count. The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth confirmed that no specific proximity or density thresholds are published for case-by-case exceptions, and the separate CDC Supermarket Vouchers category serves eight participating supermarkets with over 400 outlets.

results.title: Channelling of Additional Charge Collected under Beverage Container Return Scheme
results.summary: The Minister clarified that the 10-cent deposit under the NEA-led Beverage Container Return Scheme is refundable to consumers, not a fee for producers. The scheme operator, BCRS Ltd, an industry-led not-for-profit entity, uses collected funds to cover collection, recycling, RVM leasing, and utility payments to town councils. BCRS Ltd is funded through producer fees and recyclable sales, with no separate cost offset provided to NEA or town councils beyond these operational mechanisms. Members are directed to the combined reply from 3 February 2026 for further details on the scheme's financial structure.

results.title: Clarification by Senior Minister of State for Health
results.summary: Senior Minister of State for Health Dr Koh Poh Koon clarified the SG Arrival Card's electronic health declaration logic: individuals selecting "yes" for symptoms will be prompted to declare travel to West Bengal, whereas those selecting "no" will only be asked about pre-existing monitoring destinations. The Ministry has tightened border monitoring at migrant worker onboarding centres and alerted healthcare institutions to isolate symptomatic arrivals and provide supportive care. Officials advise monitoring symptoms for 14 days if travelers visited at-risk areas prior to arrival. The Minister stated that seeking a vaccine target is currently premature while maintaining a cautious national posture.

results.title: Complaints or Appeals Received Regarding Selection of Junior Athletes at National Sports Associations
results.summary: The Government does not track complaints or appeals regarding junior athlete selection in National Sports Associations (NSAs). Instead, NSAs must adopt merit-based criteria focusing on sporting performance and potential, excluding socio-economic background, and submit policies to Sport Singapore and the Singapore National Olympic Council for review before publication. Athletes not selected may appeal to their NSA based on new information or concerns about process fairness. Appeals are handled by independent committees separate from selection panels, with unresolved cases escalated via Sport Singapore's Alternative Dispute Resolution framework. Athletes can seek advice from their respective NSAs, Sport Singapore, or the Singapore National Olympic Council regarding selection concerns.

results.title: Composition and Expertise of Steering Committee for Singapore College of Islamic Studies
results.summary: The Acting Minister-in-charge of Muslim Affairs confirmed that the Singapore College of Islamic Studies (SCIS) Steering Committee and Board of Governors include non-Muslim members contributing expertise in higher education, social sciences, law, finance, and governance to support interdisciplinary development. All board members undergo a structured induction programme covering the College's mission, vision, and values, with ongoing engagement through events like the recent symposium on 27 January. SCIS plans to admit more than 50 madrasah students annually, expanding capacity over time, while establishing career pathways and professional development programmes beyond initial certification. The College will leverage regional partnerships with institutions in Southeast Asia and beyond, and engage professionals as resource panels for curriculum and advisory roles. Outreach efforts aim to involve the wider Singaporean community and other Institutions of Higher Learning to foster a seamless further education ecosystem.

results.title: Annual Budget Statement
results.summary: Presenting the Financial Policy for FY2026, the Government outlines a strategy to harness AI as a strategic advantage while ensuring benefits are widely shared and workers are protected. To manage structural constraints and worker anxiety, a new National AI Council will be established, and SkillsFuture Singapore and Workforce Singapore will merge into a single statutory board for seamless service delivery. Foreign worker policies are refined to maintain quality, with the EP minimum qualifying salary for new applicants rising from $5,600 to $6,000 from January 2027, while financial services sector thresholds also increase. The Budget reaffirms commitment to climate action despite global decarbonisation slowing, strengthens support for families through enhanced subsidies, and positions Singapore to navigate geopolitical uncertainties with confidence.

results.title: Concentration of Trades in Shophouses along Geylang Road
results.summary: Shophouses along Geylang Road are zoned Commercial and Hotel, permitting uses like hair salons and TCM clinics. However, the area is designated an exclusion zone prohibiting new public entertainment establishments requiring SPF licences, including pubs, bars, nightclubs, karaoke lounges, and massage establishments, to mitigate disturbances. The Minister clarified that existing structural soundproofing limits have not triggered a review of these restrictions, as the current zoning already safeguards surrounding amenities by preventing new noise-generating trades in this specific corridor.

results.title: Criteria for Location of Active Ageing Centres in New BTO Estates
results.summary: The Ministry of Health and the Agency for Integrated Care locate Active Ageing Centres (AACs) in new Build-To-Order estates using criteria that include current and projected senior numbers, local service needs, and accessibility. Demand and accessibility are assessed via administrative data and local service requirements. The Agency for Integrated Care collaborates with stakeholders to inform residents of expected operationalisation timelines for upcoming AACs. Indicative timelines are shared with residents through stakeholder engagement rather than a separate public release.

results.title: Cross-Ministry Collaboration to Tackle Problematic Gaming amongst Youths
results.summary: The Ministry of Education (MOE) leads a multi-agency framework addressing problematic gaming through preventive education, early intervention via social service agencies, and treatment referrals to the National Addiction Management Service. MOE partners with the Ministry of Social and Family Development and the Health Promotion Board to equip parents with resources for recognizing excessive gaming signs and managing screen-free activities. Regulatory measures include the 2022 Code of Practice for Online Safety and upcoming 2025 app distribution rules, though the Government does not currently engage gaming platforms directly on addictive design features, leaving such decisions to the platforms while retaining regulatory levers. The approach is calibrated by student age, focusing on safe online practices in primary school, empathy in secondary school, and cyber ethics in post-secondary levels.

results.title: Cross-agency Programmes to Deal with High Exposure to Adverse Weather Risks for Different Economic Sectors
results.summary: The Minister confirmed that while the Aviation Meteorological Programme addresses aviation-specific weather impacts, the Meteorological Service Singapore (MSS) actively collaborates with other agencies to manage risks across sectors like construction and maritime. MSS launched the Climate and Weather Research Alliance Singapore (CAWRAS) in September 2024 to leverage high-resolution modelling and artificial intelligence for enhanced weather prediction. This initiative aims to provide timely, localised weather information to protect workers and support the Maritime and Port Authority of Singapore's Port Operations Control Centres in ensuring navigation safety. Additionally, a Heat Stress Advisory launched in July 2023 assists the public with prolonged outdoor activities, demonstrating ongoing cross-agency efforts to mitigate adverse weather risks through technological advancements and stakeholder consultation.

results.title: Data for Posting Results of PSLE Students in UPLIFT Programme
results.summary: The Minister clarified that UPLIFT data is now integrated into ComLink+, a whole-of-society initiative launched in 2023 to support lower-income families. While specific PSLE posting proportions for the last five years were not provided, the Ministry reported that approximately 70% of ComLink+ families with school-going children in 2023 and 2024 had all children attending regularly and progressing to post-secondary education. The Minister attributed these outcomes to holistic wrap-around support involving family coaches, school officers, and social service agencies rather than isolated academic interventions. Future trends require more time to observe as the programme scales up, with a focus on coordinating community groups through town-level Alliance Working Groups to prevent siloed efforts and support holistic student development beyond academics.

results.title: Data on Adoption of FWA for Teachers and Ensuring Consistency of Implementation of FWA across Schools
results.summary: The Ministry of Education does not collect data on the frequency of teachers working from home, as Flexible Work Arrangements (FWA) vary by school operational needs. MOE provides guidelines while granting School Leaders autonomy to decide on local arrangements that balance student learning with teacher well-being. Feedback on implementation is channeled through regular staff engagement surveys and unions. While MOE encourages supporting feasible requests, such as adjusted start/end times or attending online meetings from home, specific FWA usage data is not tracked. Teachers in most secondary schools, junior colleges, and Millennia Institute receive at least one Home-Based Learning day per term.

results.title: Data on Amount and Destinations for Recyclables Collected from Blue Recycling Bins
results.summary: Approximately 90% of recyclables from Singapore's blue bins are exported, primarily to Asian nations. Under the Basel Convention, hazardous wastes and controlled non-hazardous items like mixed plastic require importing country consent confirming environmentally sound management. For non-convention items such as sorted metals, paper, and glass, exporters must comply with the destination country's domestic regulations. The Minister noted that metal, paper, glass, and plastics constitute the majority of collected recyclables. Verification measures ensure exports are legitimately recycled rather than subjected to overseas dumping or rejection.

results.title: Data on Feedback Received on Misleading Social Media Content in Past Five Years and Number of Cases Involving Paid Influencers or Sponsored Posts without Proper Disclosure
results.summary: From 2021 to 2025, the Advertising Standards Authority of Singapore (ASAS) received between 40 and 48 annual feedback cases regarding misleading social media content. Approximately 2% of these cases involved paid influencers or sponsored posts lacking clear disclosure, resulting in breaches of the Singapore Code of Advertising Practice. Affected parties were typically required to amend or remove the non-compliant content. The Government has not provided specific data on feedback volumes for public agencies beyond ASAS's reported figures.

results.title: Data on Illegal Use of Deregistered Vehicles, and Crime and Traffic Violations Linked to These Vehicles
results.summary: Amendments to the Road Traffic Act passed this month increase penalties for retaining or using deregistered vehicles to a first-time fine of up to $20,000 or imprisonment of up to two years. Owners must scrap or export such vehicles and provide proof within one month of deregistration, with failure constituting an offence. In 2025, more than 80 crimes and traffic violations involved these unlawfully retained vehicles. The Land Transport Authority continues to share intelligence and conduct joint operations with other agencies to prevent illegal use.

results.title: Data on Spousal Abuse Cases with Both Spouses Filing Complaints in 2024 and Safeguards to Prevent Abusers from Making Counter-reports
results.summary: In 2024, there were 2,136 spousal abuse cases, though no detailed breakdown of counter-reports or cross-complaints is available due to inconsistent agency data recording. Protection Specialist Centres and Family Service Centres apply professional frameworks, including structured interviews and case history reviews, to identify primary aggressors and mitigate coercive control tactics. These evidence-informed assessment protocols allow practitioners to make objective judgments without pre-judgement, effectively reducing the risk of abusers misusing counter-reports. The National Anti-Violence and Sexual Harassment Helpline (1800-777-0000) remains available for those seeking immediate assistance.

results.title: Data on Subscription Cancellation Complaints across Sectors for Past Five Years
results.summary: From 2021 to 2025, the Competition and Consumer Commission of Singapore received 1 to 6 annual complaints regarding difficulties cancelling recurring subscriptions or unauthorized subscriptions. The Consumers Association of Singapore recorded 49 complaints in 2024 and 44 in 2025, with over 90% resolved through negotiation or advice. The top three sectors driving these complaints are telecommunications services, gym and fitness memberships, and food delivery platform subscriptions. Most cases were settled without further follow-up after CASE intervened on behalf of consumers.

results.title: Definition and Measurable Indicators for "Good Jobs" under Economic Strategy Review
results.summary: The Ministry defines a "good job" operationally using wages as the primary, objective indicator to track job quality across sectors and occupations, while acknowledging that individual perceptions also involve career development, safety, and personal fulfilment. Key tracked metrics include labour market participation, underutilisation, and earnings, with real gross monthly income at the median rising 7.4% from 2021 to 2025. The Government will continue measuring multiple dimensions, including wage share, and aligns its approach with the OECD framework. Regarding the Economic Strategy Review's specific commitments to uplift trades and skilled roles, the Minister stated these areas remain under deliberation, and no final benchmarks or timelines have been announced for Parliament to verify.

results.title: Deployment Timeline of Two-Car Trains for Sengkang-Punggol LRT at Sengkang East and West Loops by 2026 to 2027
results.summary: By end-February 2026, seven new Light Rail Vehicles (LRVs) will be deployed on the Sengkang-Punggol LRT. The remaining 18 LRVs will be progressively rolled out from early 2027 onwards, contingent upon the completion of depot expansion works. The Acting Minister for Transport noted that the specific deployment schedule for the Sengkang East and West Loops in 2026 and 2027 will be determined based on prevailing operational requirements and ridership patterns at the time.

results.title: Effect of Rising Geopolitical Tensions, Climate Volatility and Export Restrictions on Strategies in Singapore Food Story 2.0
results.summary: Rising geopolitical tensions and climate volatility have shaped the Global Partnerships pillar of Singapore Food Story 2.0 to strengthen food trade resilience. The Minister cited recent memorandum agreements on rice trade with Vietnam and Thailand, which facilitate additional supply activation. Additionally, a joint feasibility study between Singapore and Brunei will explore establishing an Agri-Tech Food Zone to leverage mutual strengths. These measures focus on trade facilitation and information sharing to mitigate supply chain fragmentation.

results.title: Effectiveness of Car-lite Designations on Vehicle Ownership and Transit Usage since 2019 and Lessons Learnt from Implementation of Car-lite Measures
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport stated that the 16 gazetted car-lite areas, including Bayshore and Ulu Pandan, remain in development with first sites in Bayshore expected completion around 2030. Consequently, an assessment of the measures' impact on vehicle ownership and public transport usage will only occur after these areas are largely occupied. No specific lessons have been shared yet as the designated zones are not yet fully operational for evaluation.

results.title: Encouraging Exposure to Credible, Diverse Perspectives in Light of Survey Indicator that Fewer Singaporeans Regularly Accessed Diverse Viewpoints
results.summary: The Minister for Digital Development and Information acknowledged global risks of echo chambers driven by algorithms, fractured media, and AI-generated content, noting Singapore's exposure to external influences exploiting racial and religious fault lines. While the 2026 Edelman Trust Barometer indicates high trust in Public Service Media (PSM) and the Government, the Minister emphasized that no single solution exists and continuous investment is required. To counter misinformation and deepen polarization, the Government invests in PSM entities like Mediacorp and SPH Media to provide quality journalism in four official languages, alongside the National Library Board's SURE programme to strengthen information literacy. Community building initiatives under the "we first" approach further support social cohesion, enabling citizens to critically assess narratives that could strain unity. The Government will continue monitoring the information space to ensure a resilient, well-informed society during peacetime and crises.

results.title: Enhanced Consumer Protection and Standardised Credit Card Dispute Resolution across Financial Institutions
results.summary: Existing card scheme rules generally exempt customers from liability for unauthorised transactions at merchants not using 3-D Secure, provided disputes are raised promptly. The Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) mandates that financial institutions offer reporting channels, conduct prompt investigations without demanding payment of disputed amounts, and adhere to fair dispute resolution processes. Customers dissatisfied with an institution's outcome may seek mediation and adjudication through the Financial Industry Disputes Resolution Centre. While specific reporting processes vary by institution and customer segment, all entities must comply with MAS requirements ensuring fair treatment. The public is advised to safeguard card information, set lower transaction notification limits, monitor activity regularly, and immediately notify institutions of fraudulent transactions.

results.title: Enhanced Penalties against Fake Security Threats and Public Education on Reporting through Proper Channels
results.summary: The Ministry maintains that existing penalties under section 268A of the Penal Code, including a maximum of seven years' imprisonment and a $50,000 fine, remain adequate for false security threats. Under the Online Criminal Harms Act, platforms may be directed to block content disclosing such offences, with non-compliance constituting an offence. Public education on proper reporting channels is conducted via the SGSecure Movement through diverse touchpoints, including the SGSecure website and social media. The Ministry regularly refreshes these engagement strategies to ensure relevance across demographics.

results.title: Ensuring Concrete Improvements to Vehicular Safety Practices following January 2026's Voluntary Safety Time-out
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) conducted a voluntary vehicular safety time-out from 13 to 27 January 2026 following four December fatalities, issuing no inspections or enforcement actions but providing advisories. MOM performs regular inspections in high-risk sectors, conducting around 500 in January and issuing three stop-work orders and five fines for vehicle breaches. Existing Workplace Safety and Health guidelines recommend rest breaks and fatigue management, while the Productivity Solutions Grant covers up to 50% of costs for vehicle safety technologies, capping at $30,000. As of July 2025, approximately 1,000 companies have adopted such technologies with $12.5 million dispensed. MOM declined to mandate technology adoption, preferring a calibrated approach to foster a sustainable safety culture while awaiting final investigation outcomes on recent incidents.

results.title: Ensuring Energy Resilience and Supply Stability Given Increased Renewable Energy Deployment
results.summary: As of June 2025, Singapore has deployed 1.7 GWp of solar photovoltaics, progressing toward a 2 GWp target by 2030. To manage intermittency from solar generation and future regional imports, the Energy Market Authority utilizes regulation reserves and has deployed battery energy storage systems (BESS) for instantaneous supply-demand balancing. Developers of renewable projects are required to mitigate intermittency through source-level BESS or equivalent solutions. The government maintains stringent reliability standards, performs real-time monitoring of system conditions, and invests in new grid infrastructure and technologies to ensure a reliable power supply as renewable deployment increases.

results.title: Expansion of Microplastics and PFAS Monitoring Programme from Water to Sediments and Food
results.summary: The Singapore Food Agency, National Environment Agency, and Public Utilities Board continue monitoring microplastics and PFAS levels in food, drinking water, coastal waters, and sediments without recent programme expansions. Most recent data indicate that concentrations of these substances remain low or undetectable across all monitored environments. No new findings or changes to the monitoring scope have been reported by the relevant agencies.

results.title: Extending Age Limit for PHV Driver's Licence to Beyond 75 Years
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed that the upper statutory age limit for Private Hire Car Driver's Vocational Licences will remain at 75 years old. This decision follows consultations with the Singapore Medical Association, citing higher accident rates for drivers aged 70 to 74 and increasing chronic disease incidence among those over 70. While the Minister noted that drivers certified fit by doctors are considered, the current policy does not extend the limit beyond 75. The Ministry stated it will continue to periodically review the age limit in the future.

results.title: Factors Considered When Pritorising Rail Line Upgrades
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport clarified that new two-car Light Rail Vehicles (LRVs) were initially deployed to the Punggol loops in the second half of last year and to Sengkang loops since January this year to mitigate operational risks during initial trials. By end-February 2026, three and four new LRVs will be deployed to Sengkang and Punggol loops respectively. Future prioritisation for upgrades will primarily depend on the level of crowding in each loop while considering operational requirements, rather than specific ridership thresholds or infrastructure readiness assessments. The Minister noted that new LRVs were first deployed at Punggol to ensure any initial faults would not disrupt the launching of trains from the depot located at Sengkang.

results.title: Findings from 2020 Pilot to Remove CCA Selection Trials
results.summary: The 2020 pilot to remove Co-Curricular Activity (CCA) selection trials found that primary schools could successfully allocate students without trials. Currently, about two-thirds of primary schools do not conduct selection trials, while the remaining schools limit trials to oversubscribed CCAs to manage enrolment. The Ministry notes that most schools already offer recreational tiers and collaborate with SportSG for additional sports opportunities like the Sports Education Programme. While the Minister did not mandate a requirement for all schools to offer both recreational and competitive tiers, the system continues to be strengthened to ensure development opportunities for all students.

results.title: Health Screenings for Those from Countries with High-risk of Nipah Virus, and Healthcare Advisories to Doctors and Public
results.summary: On 28 January 2026, the Communicable Diseases Agency (CDA) issued clinical guidance to medical practitioners, conducted an educational webinar for primary care doctors, and activated temperature screening for ship arrivals from affected areas. The electronic Health Declaration Card within the SG Arrival Card was updated to prompt entry of previous travel destinations, including West Bengal, if symptoms are reported, while symptom-free travellers avoid excessive compliance needs. All declaration data is piped into CDA for contact tracing. The Ministry of Manpower disseminated precautionary health measures to migrant workers, employers, and dormitory operators. CDA monitors global risks and will adjust measures as needed. Local research progress on Nipah diagnostic kits, vaccines, and bio-safety level 4 laboratory capacity, along with relevant grants or subsidies, remains unaddressed in the transcript.

results.title: Higher Income Tax Payments following Change from Percentage-based to Fixed-dollar Working Mother's Child Relief
results.summary: Approximately 9,500 working mothers paid higher income tax in Year of Assessment 2025 following the shift from a percentage-based to a fixed-dollar basis for the Working Mother's Child Relief (WMCR). The Minister explained this change aims to provide equal support for children of the same order regardless of the mother's income, thereby improving the progressivity of financial assistance. While the Government cannot project additional tax revenue over the next decade due to variables like income levels and family size, the total cost of marriage and parenthood initiatives is expected to far exceed any such revenue. This measure is part of a broader package costing close to $7 billion in FY2026, which includes enhanced parental leave and the Large Families Scheme to support Singaporeans in their parenthood journey.

results.title: Identifying and Summoning Non-Singaporean Motorists for Traffic Offences Committed with Foreign-registered Vehicles
results.summary: Foreign-registered vehicles committing traffic offences in Singapore receive composition fines, and those with outstanding fines face denial of entry at land checkpoints until payment is made. The Ministry of Home Affairs is evaluating additional measures to address offences by non-resident motorists and will announce further details when ready. This enforcement mechanism ensures that vehicle owners or drivers are held accountable regardless of their residence status.

results.title: Impact of Bus Arrival Timing System Faults on Bus Services and Commuters, and Corrective Measures Taken
results.summary: Approximately 4,000 bus services experienced intermittent data transmission and missing arrival predictions due to defective software and a server failure in January 2026, though overall bus operations remained unaffected. The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed no cybersecurity concerns, attributing the issue to memory cache buildup from internal storage rather than a security breach. Post-incident measures include fixing the software to prevent recurrence and upgrading the Bus Fleet Management System, which has been in progress since 2024 to integrate improved prediction accuracy. Cross-checking predictions against actual logged timings continues to identify any future spikes in inaccurate data.

results.title: Impact of Government-issued Vouchers and Credits on 2025 GDP Growth
results.summary: Singapore's 2025 GDP growth of 5% was primarily driven by outward-oriented sectors like manufacturing and finance, which contributed 62.1% of the total growth. Support measures including Community Development Council vouchers, SG60 vouchers, and child LifeSG credits were introduced to assist households with living expenses. These consumer-facing initiatives collectively contributed approximately 0.7% to the 2025 GDP growth. The Minister did not provide estimates on the underlying growth rate without these specific measures, nor did he quantify the exact portion of growth driven solely by time-limited vouchers.

results.title: Impact of US Tariff Increases and US Export Declines on Price Pressures for Singaporeans
results.summary: Singapore's non-oil domestic exports to the US fell 9.2% year-on-year in 2025 amid a 10% baseline reciprocal tariff and sectoral levies, yet these measures are not expected to directly impact local inflation as tariffs are borne by US importers. While import prices declined to 0.9% CPI and 0.7% Core Inflation last year, the Ministry of Trade and Industry monitors macro price pressures and specific indices to identify disproportionate effects on lower-income households, noting that inflation for the lowest 20% income group was 0.6%. Officials will continue tracking import prices and inflation rates to assess indirect impacts, such as potential future price increases in US exports, and stand ready to provide targeted support if necessary.

results.title: Implication of Aquaculture Plan on Singapore Food Story 2.0
results.summary: The refreshed Singapore Aquaculture Plan (SAP), launched at the end of 2024, supports the Grow Local pillar of Singapore Food Story 2.0 through a collaborative blueprint involving government, academia, industry, and nature groups. Key initiatives include the National Broodstock Centre (NBC), which supplies genetically superior, disease-free eggs with higher grow-out rates, and the Hatchery Development and Recognition Programme (HDRP), where commercial hatcheries raise these eggs into fingerlings for farming. The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment confirmed these measures aim to uplift the sector via this multi-stakeholder approach.

results.title: Incorporating Neuro-developmental Conditions in Existing Tiered-care Mental Health Model to Ensure Holistic Care
results.summary: The current tiered-care mental health model in Singapore applies to individuals with neurodevelopmental conditions like ADHD and autism who have co-occurring mental health needs, regardless of specific diagnoses. Eligible patients can access free or subsidised community services such as REACH, CHAT, and CIHT, while public Specialist Outpatient Clinics offer means-tested subsidies of up to 70% for specialist psychiatric care. For those facing financial difficulties, medical social workers at public healthcare institutions provide assistance through MediFund. Frontline teams are supported by awareness courses and encouraged to refer clients with detected co-occurring conditions like depression to higher-tier services for co-management.

results.title: Legislating Mandatory Disclosure of Odds and Probability Ranges for Blind Box or "Gacha" Products
results.summary: The Ministry of Home Affairs and the Gambling Regulatory Authority are drafting regulations to manage gambling inducement risks associated with blind box and "gacha" products. While the suggestion for mandatory, standardised disclosure of odds and probability ranges will be considered, no specific timeline or final eligibility criteria have been announced. The current focus remains on establishing conditions for offering these products rather than implementing the specific disclosure framework proposed by the Member.

results.title: Legitimacy of Myanmar's January 2026 General Elections, Supporting ASEAN's Position of Non-recognition and Impacts on Resolving Myanmar's Political Situation
results.summary: The Government assesses that Myanmar's January 25, 2026 general election lacks legitimacy because preconditions for a legitimate government, including a cessation of hostilities and inclusive dialogue, have not been met. Singapore aligns with the ASEAN Chair's position of non-recognition and maintains Myanmar's non-political representation at ASEAN Summits and Foreign Ministers' Meetings until meaningful progress is made on the Five-Point Consensus. The conduct of the election does not alter this stance, as the consensus remains the framework for resolving the situation. Singapore continues to support ASEAN-led efforts for a Myanmar-owned resolution consistent with the Five-Point Consensus.

results.title: Local Research into Nipah Virus Diagnostic Kits and Vaccines
results.summary: The Ministry of Health funds Nipah virus research via the PREPARE programme, currently supporting six projects at Duke-NUS, NUS, and A*STAR that focus on diagnostics, therapeutics, and vaccines without using live virus. Singapore does not yet possess a Biosafety Level 4 laboratory, though the Ministry of Defence is upgrading DSO National Laboratories to this standard. No definitive timeline exists for locally developed diagnostic kits or vaccines, as research outcomes depend on scientific progress rather than fixed schedules. The Health Sciences Authority will monitor international vaccine trials but deems premature procurement unnecessary given the virus's localized nature and rare human-to-human transmission. Regional coordination with ASEAN partners and the World Health Organization continues to share genomic data and adjust national health alerts.

results.title: Managing Implementation of EIA Protocols and Requirements under High Seas Treaty and Safeguarding Security-sensitive Information
results.summary: Agencies are reviewing regulatory frameworks for High Seas Treaty activities, applying Singapore's existing EIA framework where reports are publicly available unless national security requires confidentiality. To avoid duplication, agencies will coordinate oversight rather than appointing a new centralised body. The Minister confirmed that safeguards protect sensitive information while maintaining transparency, with harmonisation of protocols across agencies currently under review. No specific timeline for full protocol harmonisation was provided.

results.title: Monitoring Rents and Profitability to Ensure Sustainability of Small Businesses, Including Heritage and City Centre Retail Shops
results.summary: Between 2019 and 2025, F&B and retail entity numbers rose by 24% and 18% respectively, exceeding population growth and contributing to profitability declines from 1.3% to 1.1% and 4.7% to 4.5% due to intensified competition. While rental cost shares fell to approximately 20% and 26% for F&B and retail sectors, non-rental costs like labour now drive pressure, necessitating innovation and productivity improvements. Enterprise Singapore supports this through the FoodX programme for central kitchen outsourcing and schemes for experiential retail formats. To aid cost management, the Government introduced a 50% Corporate Income Tax Rebate for YA 2024 and 2025, capped at $40,000 with a minimum $2,000 benefit for companies with local employees. Additionally, eligible hawkers and heartland businesses can access patronage via CDC and SG60 vouchers.

results.title: National Mindline 1771 Usage Statistics, Demographic Analysis and Service Utilisation across Support Channels and Categories
results.summary: Since launching in June 2025, National Mindline 1771 has handled over 39,000 contacts across telephone, WhatsApp, and webchat channels, with distribution of approximately 60%, 25%, and 15% respectively. About 26% of help-seekers remain anonymous, while age and gender data are often undisclosed; however, where determined, users are roughly evenly split by gender and mostly aged 20 to 35. Service needs are distributed as 10% for immediate crisis intervention, 75% for early support and counselling, and 15% for wayfinding and referrals. Most referrals direct help-seekers to Community Outreach Teams, Community Intervention Teams, and Family Counselling Centres.

results.title: Number of Fitness Corners and Public Fitness Facilities Installed and Those That Are Equipped for Strength Training
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth reported over 3,400 fitness corners installed across HDB estates, parks, and Sport-in-Precinct facilities, with most designed as multi-generational units. While many corners utilize fixed-resistance or bodyweight stations, residents can access 28 ActiveSG gyms equipped with variable resistance and free-weight machines for progressive strength training. Some ActiveSG locations also feature pneumatic technology machines offering accessible workouts for users of all abilities. The response did not specify the exact count of corners equipped with adjustable resistance machines versus those with fixed resistance.

results.title: Placement of GCE "O" Level Private Candidates in Junior Colleges
results.summary: The Minister clarified that the Joint Admissions Exercise (JAE) is open to all eligible GCE O-Level certificate holders, including private candidates, who are treated equally under the prevailing posting framework. While private candidates not participating in the JAE may apply directly to Junior Colleges, their admission is restricted to remaining vacancies after the JAE concludes. Candidates previously admitted to other institutions like polytechnics can only apply for different institution types via subsequent JAEs. The Ministry encourages all candidates seeking Junior College admission to participate in the JAE to secure placement in their first-choice courses if vacancies exist.

results.title: Plans for Onboarding Traditional Chinese Medicine Practitioners to National Electronic Health Record
results.summary: The Ministry for Health confirmed that Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) services are not licensable healthcare services under the Healthcare Services Act 2020. Consequently, data from TCM practitioners will not be contributed to the National Electronic Health Record (NEHR) system as stipulated by the Health Information Bill. Therefore, there are no plans to onboard TCM practitioners to NEHR, and no timeline or technical support for upgrading their practice management systems for this purpose will be provided. The issue of data interoperability between TCM diagnostic terminology and Western medical records within NEHR does not apply given the exclusion of TCM data from the system.

results.title: Progress Toward 4,000-flat Target for Parenthood Provisional Housing Scheme
results.summary: The Minister for National Development confirmed that HDB has met its target of supplying 4,000 units under the Parenthood Provisional Housing Scheme (PPHS) by the end of 2025. Allocation priority is given to households with at least one child aged 18 and below, married couples expecting a child, followed by married couples without children and fiancé-fiancée couples. Recent exercises saw all highest-priority households invited to select flats, with approximately 30% of the next tier also selected. The Ministry assesses that around 4,000 units represent the appropriate steady-state supply, noting that additional Shorter Waiting Time flats are being ramped up concurrently. While the Ministry continues to seek available Selective En Bloc Redevelopment Scheme (SERS) vacant flats, Lease Buyback Scheme (LBS) returned units are reserved for resale rather than rental. As the building programme has caught up, the Ministry anticipates achieving a steady state for PPHS flats alongside increased Shorter Waiting Time availability.

results.title: Projected Expenditure on Government's Pursuit of Global Leadership in Growth Areas and AI-empowered Economy
results.summary: Under Singapore's National AI Strategy 2.0, the Government pursues global leadership in growth areas through investments in public and private sectors, prioritizing performance, security, resilience, and cost-effectiveness. The Minister stated that data sovereignty and public interest are safeguarded by the Personal Data Protection Act, sector-specific regulations, and the Health Information Bill, which require legitimate purposes, Ministerial authorization, and contractual agreements for sharing public sector data. While the Government partners with international entities to build skills and create local jobs, specific commercial models and fees for using public datasets remain use-case specific and driven by relevant agencies. Further details on expenditure and the AI-empowered economy will be provided in the upcoming 2026 Budget Statement and Committee of Supply debates.

results.title: Proposal for Singular Queue Number and Booking Appointment to Select Two Units Concurrently under Family Care Scheme (Joint Balloting)
results.summary: The Family Care Scheme (Joint Balloting), effective from the October 2025 sales exercise, replaces the Multi-Generation Priority Scheme by allowing parent-child applicants to jointly apply for two units in the same Build-To-Order project. Unlike the previous scheme, successful applicants now receive distinct queue numbers and separate booking appointments to select from all available units, offering greater choice. While the Housing and Development Board does not assign a single joint queue number, it exercises flexibility on a case-by-case basis for applicants facing limited choices towards the end of the booking exercise.

results.title: Reducing Legal, Administrative and Financial Barriers Experienced by Unmarried and Low-income Mothers Formalising Legal Relationship with Biological Children
results.summary: The Minister stated that the adoption process for unmarried mothers adopting their biological children remains consistent to safeguard child welfare, involving steps like terminating the birth father's legal rights. While acknowledging financial challenges, MSF offers means-tested fee waivers for its processes. Unmarried mothers may also access Legal Aid Bureau assistance for court fees and legal costs or represent themselves in court. No new simplified or affordable pathway was announced, as the existing framework applies uniformly to all adoption cases regardless of marital status.

results.title: Registration Process Support Measures for Healthcare Professional Candidates for Past Five Years
results.summary: Over the past five years, 3% to 5% of healthcare professionals failed to obtain full registration by the end of their fixed supervision and training term. The Ministry supports these individuals through standardised training programmes, competency frameworks, and regular performance reviews to identify areas for improvement. Those who do not attain full registration may continue practising safely under supervision while working towards meeting the requirements for independent practice. Additional targeted support and training are provided where needed to help them progress towards full registration.

results.title: Regulatory Review of Consumer Safeguards in Insurance and E-commerce Partnership Models
results.summary: The Minister stated that current consumer safeguards under the Insurance Act are adequate for financial institutions distributing products via e-commerce platforms. General insurance agents must be registered with the General Insurance Association's Agents' Registration Board (ARB) and comply with its rules on professionalism, conduct, and competency. Agents failing to meet these standards face disciplinary actions, including registration cancellation. Institutions are also expected to assess whether e-commerce platforms can comply with their own digital advertising practices. The government will continue monitoring the landscape and introducing new safeguards if necessary.

results.title: Response Procedures to Address Residential Secondhand Tobacco Smoke Complaints and Consideration of Stronger Intervention for Persistent Cases
results.summary: The National Environment Agency and relevant agencies issue advisories to encourage considerate smoking behavior upon receiving secondhand smoke complaints. Affected residents may access free mediation services at the Community Mediation Centre for persistent disputes. As a final recourse, residents can file claims with the Community Disputes Resolution Tribunal. The government will periodically review policies while monitoring international best practices. No specific regulatory measures or structured interventions were confirmed for high-density estates beyond existing advisory and mediation frameworks.

results.title: Review of Retail Mix in Toa Payoh to Serve Younger Families Residing in Upcoming Toa Payoh East, Caldecott and Mount Pleasant BTO Estates
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that the Housing and Development Board (HDB) regularly reviews commercial facility planning to ensure retail mixes meet resident needs. For upcoming developments in Toa Payoh East, Caldecott, and Mount Pleasant, HDB evaluates the necessity for new retail based on demographic profiles and existing regional provision. Specific plans for new shopping centres in these areas will be announced when details are ready. The Ministry has not confirmed a specific timeline or immediate commitment to new amenities beyond this ongoing evaluation process.

results.title: Role-Based Access to National Electronic Health Records for Registered Community Optometrists
results.summary: The Ministry clarified that registered optometrists in Healthcare Services Act-licensed institutions may access the National Electronic Health Record system to support patient care, while those in non-licensed institutions are not currently required to submit health information to the system. This distinction aims to facilitate care coordination for licensed providers but does not extend role-based access or submission requirements to non-licensed community optometrists at this time. The Minister noted that information from non-HCSA institutions remains outside the mandatory submission framework for the National Electronic Health Record.

results.title: Sales Volume of Lease Buyback Scheme Flats and Breakdown of Average Remaining Lease by Flat Type
results.summary: As of end-2025, approximately 16,000 households have participated in the Lease Buyback Scheme (LBS) since its launch on 1 March 2009. The scheme allows eligible senior homeowners to sell a portion of their remaining lease to HDB while retaining a lease sufficient for the youngest owner to reach age 95. Retained lease durations generally range from 15 to 35 years, varying based on the homeowner's age and individual circumstances at the time of application.

results.title: SingPass Login Success Rates and Application Abandonment Patterns for CDRT Claims
results.summary: Between 2023 and 2024, approximately 394 claims were filed via SingPass or CJTSPass for Community Disputes Resolution Tribunals. The Community Justice and Tribunals System does not track the annual number of unique logins, the count of successfully submitted claims, the specific stage with the highest abandonment rate, or the median submission time. Consequently, the Minister could not provide data on login success rates, abandonment patterns, or processing durations as requested.

results.title: SkillsFuture Credit Utilisation across Income Bands, Housing Types and Course Areas in 2025 and Further Strategies to Improve Utilisation Rates
results.summary: As at end-2025, the most popular SkillsFuture Credit courses covered Information and Communication, Transportation and Storage, Advertising, Sales and Marketing, and Food and Beverages. The Ministry does not track utilisation by individual income or housing type but observes that adult learners prefer online learning to accommodate busy schedules. Measures such as the training allowance support mid-careerists in acquiring skills requiring longer-form training. SkillsFuture Singapore collaborates with community partners, the Labour Movement, and self-help groups to raise awareness among less digitally savvy individuals, aiming to ensure relevant training courses are accessible to lower-income and less-upskilled segments.

results.title: SkillsFuture Credit Utilisation for Online Subscription-based Learning Packages in Past Two Years
results.summary: From 2024 to 2025, approximately 17% of SkillsFuture Credit (Base-Tier) claims were for online subscription-based learning packages, which typically offer up to one year of access. SkillsFuture Singapore is currently discussing with providers to share subscriber usage data, aiming to better understand learning needs and interests. The Ministry encourages individuals to take ownership of their skills development and plan the meaningful use of their credit. While the proportion of claims is tracked, the transcript does not specify if subsequent utilisation after the subscription period is formally monitored.

results.title: Spotting System Faults in AVs and Strengthening Investigation and Accountability Frameworks as AV Deployment Expands
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority, in partnership with the Traffic Police and fleet operators, investigates Autonomous Vehicle (AV) incidents under the Road Traffic Act 1961 and the Road Traffic (Autonomous Motor Vehicles) Rules 2017. While the regulatory sandbox currently governs trials, the Government is collaborating with unions and industry stakeholders to review accountability frameworks. This review aims to introduce AV-specific legislation that clearly defines the responsibilities of key actors. The process seeks to strengthen investigation protocols and safeguard supervising workers as AV adoption expands.

results.title: Strengthening Safety and Handover Protocols for Deployment of Self-Driving Shuttle Buses
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority requires Autonomous Vehicle operators to submit detailed safety plans covering operator roles, manual takeover conditions, and incident response before public road deployment. Trials are governed by the Road Traffic Act 1961 and must include a passenger-free familiarisation phase to calibrate vehicles to local conditions while operators gain practical experience. Operators must maintain liability insurance covering third-party death, bodily injury, and property damage throughout the trial duration. The Government is currently reviewing liability and insurance regimes holistically with industry and legal stakeholders, with potential for new AV-specific legislation.

results.title: Structured Intervention Pathways for Managing Serious Sexual or Violence Cases, Especially Those Involving Children or Sustained Pattern of Harm
results.summary: The Minister clarified that children below the Minimum Age of Criminal Responsibility (MACR) are not criminally liable, with Police prioritizing safety and rehabilitation through existing referral mechanisms. These pathways include school disciplinary or restorative actions, Family Service Centre support for family functioning, and Institute of Mental Health assessments for mental disorders. For serious behavioural challenges, parents may apply for a Family Guidance Order, enabling the Youth Court to mandate counselling, statutory supervision, or admission to a home gazetted under the Children and Young Persons Act. Schools and agencies collaborate with parents to reinforce home support, ensuring a structured intervention approach for cases involving children below the MACR with multiple victim reports or sustained harm patterns.

results.title: Study and Mitigation of Midge Population Problem at Bedok Reservoir since November 2025
results.summary: The midge population at Bedok Reservoir from November 2025 to January 2026 is higher than previous years. The Public Utilities Board (PUB) confirmed that current biological larvicides remain effective and has no plans to rotate chemical controls. In response, PUB increased biological larvicide application and fogging frequency. LED spotlights are retained to concentrate midges for targeted fogging, though their specific effectiveness in reducing residential counts has not been quantified. PUB will continue monitoring the situation and reviewing measures as needed.

results.title: Ten-Year Breakdown of Full-time National Servicemen Numbers across SAF, SPF and SCDF
results.summary: The Minister for Defence clarified that year-by-year enlistment figures are less meaningful than long-term trends, noting that over the last decade, more than 95% of Full-time National Servicemen consistently complete their two-year terms. The small minority who do not finish their service are primarily due to medical reasons. Specific numerical breakdowns by agency (SAF, SPF, SCDF) and second-year counts were not provided in the response.

results.title: Tracking of Recurring Police Reports against Business Sales Practices in Past Five Years to Identify Potential Criminal Conduct
results.summary: The Police do not track reports of cases deemed civil in nature. Instead, case-by-case assessments are conducted on the facts, with the Attorney-General's Chambers consulted when necessary to determine if conduct is criminal and prosecutable. Consequently, there is no aggregate data on business entities subject to recurring reports that were subsequently classified as civil disputes. The Minister clarified that the decision to prosecute depends on specific factual assessments rather than a tracking mechanism for repeated civil complaints.

results.title: Traffic Accidents along Tanah Merah Coast Road and Proportion of Accidents Involving Cyclists
results.summary: Between 2020 and 2025, Tanah Merah Coast Road averaged around 20 annual accidents causing injuries or fatalities, with approximately nine involving cyclists. Primary causes include disorderly cycling, failure to keep a proper lookout, and lack of vehicle control by both cyclists and motorists. The Traffic Police conduct regular enforcement at hotspots, while the Traffic Police and Land Transport Authority collaborate with the Singapore Cycling Federation and People's Association to promote safety. These agencies disseminate guidelines on safe behaviour for motorists and cyclists to prevent future incidents along this stretch.

results.title: Update on Mandate for National Space Agency of Singapore
results.summary: The National Space Agency of Singapore will not prioritise developing domestic launch capabilities for small payloads or microsatellites due to land constraints and air congestion. Instead, the agency will leverage existing commercial launch services from overseas spaceports in French Guiana, India, Japan, and the United States. While partnerships to maintain resilient access will continue, the agency remains open to supporting emerging launch technologies that demonstrate commercial potential and strategic value for Singapore.

results.title: Update on Progress of Train Lines Under Extension and Construction
results.summary: Tunnel strengthening works must be completed before Circle Line Stage 6 testing resumes, with an expected opening in the first half of 2026 if the schedule is met. The Thomson-East Coast Line Stage 5 and Downtown Line 3 extension are projected to open in the second half of 2026. Specific opening dates and required service adjustments will be announced upon readiness. Further details on these timelines will be provided when available.

results.title: Video Consultations for Non-emergency Acute Conditions at All Polyclinics
results.summary: Video consultations at polyclinics are currently limited to follow-ups for minor acute conditions, test result reviews, or recovery monitoring for patients with prior in-person attendance; new or worsening conditions identified during teleconsultations require immediate in-person assessment. SingHealth Polyclinics pilot offers first-attendance acute video consultations, but there are no immediate plans to expand this service across all polyclinics or to the West and Central clusters, as other clusters prioritize chronic disease management. The 18-year age threshold for the pilot reflects a cautious approach to defining adulthood, while MOH guidelines emphasize provider judgement over prescriptive rules to ensure patient safety and professional duty fulfillment without constraining clinical discretion.
